user_message: Working on Consolidated Messenger's QBR preparation. Got the exec meeting notes -- help me build the analysis.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-19 10:46 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for QBR preparation. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
Date: 2024-02-02 14:10 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Consolidated Messenger

There's been a change in our timeline for QBR preparation. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 14:12 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-N trap (No native 'commitment-to-milestone' workflow primitive); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-N failure mode (No native 'commitment-to-milestone' workflow primitive)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-N -- No native 'commitment-to-milestone' workflow primitive
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
